texto: And you realize that there’s no such thing as an individual, we’re just all a collection of each other’s influence on each other. Everyone says things to each other, the television, your parents, your friends, that’s all we are, is a collection of intermingling ideas as a collective.
explicacao: Citação de Matt Bellamy sobre temas ligados a amizade. “And you realize that there’s no such thing as an individual, we’re just all a collection of each …” articula amizade, collectiveness, ideas em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como termino e ideas.

frase_original: And you realize that there’s no such thing as an individual, we’re just all a collection of each other’s influence on each other. Everyone says things to each other, the television, your parents, your friends, that’s all we are, is a collection of intermingling ideas as a collective.

texto: I've got my interests and my life experiences as I'm putting lyrics together. And if you start looking at patterns, you start thinking, 'Well, what am I really singing about here?' A lot of it seems to be a battle for some freedom against oppressive forces. That seems to be a theme in a lot of the albums I've done.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Matt Bellamy que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “I've got my interests and my life experiences as I'm putting lyrics together. And if you start lo…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, vida, together, com foco em together e thinking.
